factors.name: Hot CPI Print
factors.summary: US May CPI +4.2% YoY vs +4.2% expected; headline accelerated from 3.8% prior. Persistent price pressure, especially energy-driven, removes near-term Fed cut expectations and extends 'higher-for-longer' real yields regime, compressing long-duration growth multiples critical to Nasdaq 100 mega-cap valuations.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising
factors.summary: Hot inflation data alongside strong USD (DXY >100) implies real yields moving higher, directly bearish for duration-heavy tech stocks that dominate NQ. Long-term growth equities extremely sensitive to 10Y real yield expansion.

factors.name: US-Iran Escalation & Strait of Hormuz Closure
factors.summary: Fresh US strikes on Iran, Iran's closure of Strait of Hormuz to all vessels, and targeting of naval/air defense assets represent material supply-chain and inflation risks. Oil spiked on supply disruption fears; stagflation concerns rising. Historical playbook: geopolitical shocks + energy supply cuts weigh on high-beta growth equities and tech sentiment.

factors.name: Oracle Heavy Capex Guidance & Debt Raise
factors.summary: Oracle earnings beat but stock slid on plans to raise $40B debt+equity for AI/datacenter buildout and negative free cash flow. Signals hyperscaler AI capex cycle intensifying while balance sheets deteriorate; investors pivoting away from 'growth at any cost' narrative on rising rates.

factors.name: Semis & Mega-Cap Tech Correction Phase
factors.summary: MarketWatch reported semis (Micron, Intel) dragging tech sector into new bearish phase; pullback has 'graduated' to official correction. Validates broader NDX downtrend and suggests institutional rotation out of richly-valued growth into value/defensive.

factors.name: 24h Down -2.17% and Intraday Down -0.48%
factors.summary: Nasdaq 100 already down 2.17% in 24h indicates momentum is in downward phase; move is already extended, suggesting late-stage of selloff rather than early entry point. Participants re-pricing inflation and geopolitical risk.

factors.name: Gold, Commodities, Safe-Haven Bid
factors.summary: Gold broke March lows to $4,050, contradicting typical flight-to-safety narrative on geopolitical escalation (usually gold rallies). Suggests inflation/real-yield concern overriding geopolitical premium. Oil spiked +2% on Hormuz closure but equities fell harder, indicating risk-off dominance.

factors.name: Upcoming Data & Events
factors.summary: Light Asian economic calendar June 11; major catalysts (FOMC, next CPI, NFP) still days away. Markets pricing in higher-for-longer rates; no imminent dovish surprise expected. Fed chair Kevin Warsh reportedly comfortable with elevated inflation target (Trump stated +4.2% acceptable).

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is grinding lower in Phase 2 of a multi-day correction driven by a confluence of macro headwinds: hot CPI print (+4.2% YoY vs expectations), rising real yields, fresh US-Iran military escalation closing the Strait of Hormuz, and investor disappointment in mega-cap tech earnings (Oracle's heavy capex burden). The semis/growth sector has officially entered correction territory per recent reports. Despite geopolitical risk typically supporting safe havens, gold and long-duration assets are being sold alongside equities, indicating the inflation/real-yield regime shift is the dominant driver. A 2.17% 24h decline and already-extended intraday downside suggests we're in late-stage selloff momentum rather than a fresh entry point for bulls.
catalysts_ahead: Producer Price Index (PPI) — timing TBD, will reinforce or challenge inflation narrative
Fed speakers/Warsh comments — market parsing inflation tolerance
Continued Iran-US developments (escalation or deal headlines) — oil/equity volatility spillover
Mega-cap tech earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA if not yet reported) — guidance on AI capex and demand
10Y Treasury yield move (real yield proxy) — critical for NDX duration compression
FOMC meeting (date TBD, likely next 2-3 weeks) — rate path re-evaluation
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran talks resume and Hormuz closure is lifted, oil could collapse and risk-off premium would evaporate, allowing tech to stabilize and rally. If inflation data rolls over unexpectedly or Fed signals dovish pivot, real yields compress and Nasdaq multiples re-expand. Trump's stated comfort with 4% inflation could signal policy tolerance for lower rates than priced; this would be BULLISH for NDX. Conversely, if geopolitical escalation spreads beyond Iran or energy prices spike further, stagflation fears intensify and growth equities face additional headwinds.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended after -2.17% 24h decline. Wait for stabilization and intraday retest of near-term support (~28200–28000) before reconsidering shorts; alternatively, watch for reversal patterns and upticks in VXN as potential bull-trap signals. Do not chase shorts into gap opens lower.

factors.name: US-Iran Military Escalation
factors.summary: US launched fresh strikes on Iran for second consecutive day; Iran closed Strait of Hormuz to all vessels and attacked multiple Gulf states (Jordan, Kuwait, Bahrain). This marks active disruption to ~20% of global oil flows and elevated geopolitical risk premium, typically compressing growth multiples.

factors.name: Oil & Commodity Spike
factors.summary: WTI jumped to ~$91/bbl; Brent climbing on Hormuz closure threat. Higher energy costs feed inflation expectations and margin compression for tech-heavy NDX, which carries long-duration multiples most sensitive to real yield re-pricing.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations Rising
factors.summary: News cites US inflation topping 4% (Cointelegraph, 2026-06-11T04:51); ECB preparing first rate hike since 2023 due to Iran-war inflation. Real yields likely to rise, directly bearish for high-multiple growth stocks that dominate Nasdaq 100.

factors.name: Tech Earnings & AI Capex Concerns
factors.summary: Oracle stock slid post-earnings despite beating estimates due to surging AI costs and negative free cash flow; raising $20bn more capital (Bloomberg 2026-06-11T00:15). Signals rising capex burdens and margin pressure across hyperscalers, weighing on MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN guidance forward.

factors.name: Semiconductor & Micro Weakness
factors.summary: Micron, Intel dragged tech into correction phase (MarketWatch 2026-06-10T22:58). Copper fell to 3-week low on Middle East jitters. Semiconductor supply chain sentiment deteriorating amid geopolitical risk.

factors.name: Risk-Off Flows & Safe-Haven Demand
factors.summary: Gold bounced but remains below March low after technical breakdown; crypto BTC/XRP under pressure on macro headwinds. Risk assets broadly under pressure; USD holding near 100.00 DXY despite safe-haven bid suggesting equity liquidation over FX rotation.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Oversubscription (Positive Offset)
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO 4x oversubscribed (Bloomberg 2026-06-11T01:15), suggesting retail appetite remains; however, legal experts warn of limited investor rights, tempering longer-term enthusiasm.

factors.name: Recent Price Action Already Down -2.17% in 24h
factors.summary: 24h decline of -2.17% indicates move is already in progress post-Friday shock; current -0.48% 4h change shows near-term stabilization but downtrend intact. This is PHASE_2/3, not fresh catalyst.

factors.name: ECB Rate Decision Today (12:15 GMT)
factors.summary: ECB expected to hike for first time since 2023 (Bloomberg 2026-06-11T04:15). Tighter global financial conditions + higher rates weigh on duration-sensitive equities and growth multiples.

factors.name: Nasdaq Recovery Attempt Capped
factors.summary: Investing.com notes Nasdaq futures bouncing but not fully repaired; key resistance zone 28,850-28,900 must hold for recovery to be convincing. Rejection there would confirm downtrend continuation, consistent with PHASE_2 sell-off.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is in an intermediate sell-off phase (-2.17% in 24h) triggered by US-Iran military escalation, Strait of Hormuz closure threat, and surging inflation expectations. Tech earnings (Oracle, Micron, Intel) are showing capex bloat and margin pressure, while commodity spikes (oil +$90/bbl, gold technical breakdown) signal real yield re-pricing headwinds for high-multiple growth. The index bounced modestly in early Asia but is testing recovery resistance at 28,850-28,900; rejection there would confirm the downtrend. ECB rate hike decision at 12:15 GMT and looming Fed re-pricing risk cement near-term bearish bias.
catalysts_ahead: ECB Rate Decision & Press Conference (12:15 GMT today, 2026-06-11)
US PPI report (if scheduled today/tomorrow)
Continued US-Iran military/diplomatic developments (Strait of Hormuz, ceasefire talks)
Oil inventory data & Energy sector guidance
Fed speakers & rate expectations revision
Mega-cap earnings guidance updates (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN cloud capex commentary)
Nasdaq 100 component reaction to inflation re-pricing
screening.risk_warning: INVALIDATES SELL if: (1) US-Iran ceasefire announced + Strait of Hormuz reopens + oil crashes >5%; (2) ECB delays hike or sounds dovish, sparking safe-haven unwind and risk-on rally; (3) Nasdaq rallies convincingly through 28,900 resistance and closes above prior week high; (4) major tech earnings beat expectations with strong forward guidance on cloud/AI demand despite capex; (5) Fed speakers signal data-dependent pause or cuts coming, re-compressing real yields.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid chasing short here; move already -2.17% down and in PHASE_2. Wait for NDX to either break decisively below 28,350 support (confirm bearish breakdown) or rally back to 28,850-28,900 resistance and fail (confirm reversal). If ECB hike triggers fresh risk-off, look for short entry on any relief bounce into resistance. For longs, require close above 28,900 + positive divergence in VXN (falling volatility) to signal reversal conviction.

factors.name: Tech earnings vacuum + macro uncertainty
factors.summary: No major mega-cap tech earnings catalysts mentioned in the news. Without AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA guidance, the NDX lacks primary price drivers. Current weakness (-2.17% in 24h) reflects retreat into uncertainty.

factors.name: Fed policy uncertainty into FOMC
factors.summary: US CPI came in 'mostly in line with expectations' per FXStreet; market still pricing 24 bps tightening by year-end (down from 25 bps). FOMC is next week—no imminent pin-risk today, but longer-duration growth equities remain sensitive to real yields.

factors.name: Real yields (TIPS proxy) and long-duration tech sensitivity
factors.summary: Gold broke below March lows ($4,099) and 200-day MA, signaling rising real yields or risk-off. This is typically BEARISH for high-multiple tech; if real yields are rising, duration-heavy NDX faces headwinds.

factors.name: ECB rate hike (25 bps to 2.25%) + guidance focus
factors.summary: ECB hike is fully priced per Commerzbank; focus now on forward guidance and growth downgrade risk in new projections. European weakness may weigh on global risk sentiment and US multinational earnings, but NDX benefit from USD strength (international revenue headwind) is mixed.

factors.name: US-Iran escalation risk, oil volatility
factors.summary: Iran closed Strait of Hormuz; Brent crude climbed toward $95/bbl. Geopolitical premium and energy cost inflation create headwinds for margin-sensitive tech sectors. Ceasefire talks still on track per CNN, limiting panic, but uncertainty remains.

factors.name: AI mega-cap IPO frenzy (SpaceX imminent)
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO (likely $1.75T+ valuation) expected this week; Nasdaq futures bounced +1.3% at open per Investinglive. Mega-listing momentum may provide near-term liquidity and sentiment support if IPO is well-received; however, execution risk is high.

factors.name: China e-commerce selloff + emerging market weakness
factors.summary: EM stocks dropped for 6th time in 7 days; Chinese e-commerce hit by regulatory censure. NDX exposure to China slowdown via MSFT/TSLA/GOOGL and global growth concerns is non-trivial.

factors.name: Corporate AI capex trends and 'price war' narrative
factors.summary: ChatGPT pricing report suggests OpenAI considering discounts amid Anthropic competition; 'tokenmaxxing' complaints and cooling AI adoption pace per MarketWatch. Hyperscaler capex growth may moderate, pressuring NVDA and cloud infrastructure narrative.

factors.name: Dollar strength into FOMC
factors.summary: USD consolidating since Monday; TD Securities expects broad uptrend intact into FOMC next week. Stronger dollar pressures international revenue for NDX multinationals, but also supports nominal equity multiples.

factors.name: VIX / Volatility and options positioning
factors.summary: No explicit VXN or dealer gamma data in news; however, 24h selloff and structural breakdown in gold/risk assets suggests elevated vol regime. Tech equity options likely priced for continued chop.

key_levels.support: 28400–28500 (today's floor area); 28200–28300 (weekly MA support); 27800–28000 (longer-term structural break target if weakness extends)
key_levels.resistance: 28850–28900 (key zone per Investinglive); 29000–29100 (prior swing highs); 29500 (psychological round level)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq-100 remains in a post-selloff chop phase following Friday's shock decline (-2.17% in 24h). While futures bounced on SpaceX IPO enthusiasm and dovish ceasefire narrative, the underlying structure is damaged: gold broke below March lows, emerging markets are under pressure, and tech earnings catalysts are absent ahead of FOMC next week. Real yields appear to be rising (gold weakness), which pressures long-duration growth multiples. ECB hike today is fully priced and unlikely to shock, but forward guidance and growth downgrades could worsen EM/global risk sentiment.
catalysts_ahead: ECB policy announcement & forward guidance (12:15 GMT today) — focus on 2026–2028 inflation projections
SpaceX IPO filing/pricing (today or tomorrow) — mega-cap AI/tech sentiment test
US PPI (Producer Price Index) — macro inflation confirmation, impacts real yields
FOMC meeting (next week, ~12 June) — rate expectations reset and dot plot update
NVDA / MSFT / GOOGL / AAPL earnings cycle (June–July) — hyperscaler capex and AI demand guidance
US-Iran diplomatic resolution or escalation (next 48–72h) — oil price and risk appetite driver
screening.risk_warning: If SpaceX IPO trades strong and mega-cap tech earnings beat expectations with hawkish capex guidance, risk-on sentiment could flip and drive a retest of 28850–28900 support break. Conversely, if FOMC signals tighter-for-longer policy, real yields spike further, and EM weakness spreads to developed markets, NDX could accelerate downside toward 27800–28000. Geopolitical escalation in Middle East (Strait of Hormuz blockade sustained) could shock oil and CRB higher, forcing real yield spike and multiple compression.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE 2 (underway post-Friday selloff); confidence is low due to lack of earnings catalyst and high macro uncertainty into FOMC. Wait for NDX to test 28400–28500 support or retest 28850 resistance before taking directional entries. If SpaceX IPO is priced aggressively high and opens strong, consider long scalps into resistance; if gold remains broken, avoid aggressive longs until real yields stabilize.

factors.name: US PPI Inflation Surprise
factors.summary: PPI +6.5% YoY vs +6.4% expected; +1.1% MoM vs +0.7% expected — fastest pace since Nov 2022. Pipeline inflation re-ignites growth multiple compression risk for long-duration tech.

factors.name: Real Yields & Long-Duration Multiple Pressure
factors.summary: ECB hiked 25bp; higher-for-longer rate trajectory now embedded. Gold fell to YTD low; silver hit multi-month lows. Real rate expectations rising → tech valuations under pressure.

factors.name: Chip Sector (NVDA, ASML, Semiconductors)
factors.summary: ASML, Lam Research hitting all-time highs; Samsung +150% YTD (retail-driven). Chip strength is carrying NQ intraday bounce. But insider trading probe on Samsung clouds sentiment.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Hype & FOMO
factors.summary: $70B+ retail orders reported; Evercore notes 'wild-eyed FOMO' ahead of Friday debut. Risk-on sentiment lifting mega-cap tech & NDX. But IPO demand can be volatile post-launch.

factors.name: Middle East Escalation & Energy Shock
factors.summary: Trump threatens Kharg Island seizure; Iran closes Strait of Hormuz; oil supply fear spiking (cost shock for businesses & consumers). ECB explicitly citing conflict drag on growth. Stagflation risk elevated.

factors.name: Fed Policy Divergence (ECB vs Fed)
factors.summary: ECB raised rates into supply shock; Fed still on hold. Market now pricing sticky inflation & potential Fed reversal of rate-cut narrative. Growth outlook uncertainty rising.

factors.name: Jobless Claims & Labor Market
factors.summary: Initial claims 229K vs 219K expected; continuing claims 1.795M vs 1.780M — marginally hotter. Labor market soft but not broken; limits Fed's urgency to cut.

factors.name: World Bank Downgrades Global Growth
factors.summary: World Bank cuts 2026 global GDP to 2.5% from 2.6%; Eurozone to 0.8%; cites Iran war supply disruption. Two-thirds of economies seeing deteriorated outlook. Risk-off headwind for growth equities.

factors.name: Big Tech Concentration & Rotation Fatigue
factors.summary: Nomura's Charlie McElligott notes 'Big Tech preventing new stock-market highs' as investors waking up to concentration risks & AI valuation extremes. Breadth weakness despite NQ bounce.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (USD-CAD to new 2026 highs)
factors.summary: USD/CAD at 1.3990 (new 2026 high); risk-off sentiment & safe-haven flows supporting USD. Helps large-cap US tech with international revenue, but headwind for EM growth plays.

factors.name: Intraday Bounce Off Morning Lows
factors.summary: NQ recovering after two straight down days; chip strength providing technical support. But move is partial retracement, not breakout. Phase 2 bounce into potential resistance.

key_levels.resistance: 20,700–20,850 (recent range high; must break to confirm recovery)

screening.narrative: NAS100 faces a crucial inflection: hot PPI (+1.1% MoM) and ECB's hawkish pivot are reigniting real-rate and stagflation concerns, compressing long-duration growth multiples. SpaceX IPO hype & chip sector strength (ASML, NVDA, Lam Research ATHs) are providing tactical FOMO-driven bounce, but broader growth outlook — from World Bank, ECB guidance, and Trump's Iran escalation — is deteriorating. The market is caught between AI/mega-cap FOMO (Phase 2 upside momentum intraday) and inflation/growth headwinds (structural bearish drivers). Concentration risk in Big Tech is becoming a ceiling.
catalysts_ahead: SpaceX IPO Friday (6/12) — massive retail demand event, sentiment swing risk
Fed speakers (Barkin, Kashkari, others) — any hawkish surprise could reset rate-cut expectations
Next CPI/PCE data (late June) — will determine if Fed acknowledges sticky inflation
Iran military escalation updates — real-time risk to oil/commodity prices and growth forecasts
NVDA earnings & hyperscaler guidance (TBD) — AI capex commentary critical for NDX valuation
European PMI, ECB follow-ups — growth stall confirmation or stabilization
screening.risk_warning: Bias flips decisively BULLISH if: (1) energy shock contained & oil falls sharply, (2) Fed signals rate cuts despite higher inflation (dovish pivot), (3) SpaceX IPO pops massively & broadens equity sentiment, (4) Big Tech earnings beat & raise guidance. Bias flips BEARISH if: (1) PPI remains hot into summer & Fed forced to re-hike, (2) Iran war escalates further, (3) ECB growth forecasts fall again, (4) NVDA/hyperscaler capex guidance disappoints.
screening.entry_guidance: Intraday bounce is Phase 2 recovery; move extended into SPX rally — avoid chasing. Wait for retest of 20,450–20,550 (intermediate support/retracement zone) OR break above 20,850 resistance (requires confirmation of growth narrative shift). Real risk event is SpaceX IPO Friday; hedge/reduce longs if holding into close Thursday.

factors.name: Iran Deal Relief & De-Escalation
factors.summary: Trump cancelled scheduled Iran strikes after announcing agreement; major geopolitical risk-off catalyst removed. Market surged immediately with Nasdaq +1.8%, S&P +1.3%, Russell +2.4% on this news; reduces oil/energy inflation tail risk and stabilizes growth multiple repricing.

factors.name: Hot PPI Inflation Data
factors.summary: US producer prices rose 1.1% in May vs 0.7% expected (per Dow Jones consensus), fastest pace in 3+ years, driven by energy fallout from Iran tensions. This initially pressures Fed rate expectations and real yields, which compress long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Real Yields / 10Y Nominal
factors.summary: 10Y treasury auction saw 1.2 bps tail (30-year sold at 5.020% vs 5.008% WI, prior 5.046%), signalling softer demand in a world of rising fiscal spending and tech AI capex saturation. Initial risk but deal-driven rally may have offset some hawkish pressure.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings Window
factors.summary: Oracle tumbled 11% post-earnings on negative free cash flow and $95B capex raise despite beat, raising questions about AI profitability and capex durability. However, Oracle bonds rallied, indicating debt confidence; mixed signal on tech valuation sustainability.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Hype & Retail FOMO
factors.summary: SpaceX targeting $1.8T valuation with $70B retail order flow; IPO priced to go live Friday. Massive leverage to risk sentiment but potential market-neutral allocation effect; BlackRock seeking $5B; hyperscale fund deployment risk remains fluid.

factors.name: Dollar Direction & DXY
factors.summary: Iran de-escalation and potential oil supply normalization weaken near-term USD upside; however, higher-for-longer rates and Fed repricing from hot PPI still support dollar. Mixed cross-currents; USD strength has been headwind for NQ international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Fed Policy Pivot Timing
factors.summary: ECB hiked 25bp on Thursday; IMF says more hikes needed if energy stays elevated. Fed still data-dependent; hot PPI may delay summer cuts. Uncertainty on timing keeps rate-sensitive mega-cap multiples under pressure; no major FOMC scheduled in next 48h.

factors.name: Semiconductor / AI Capex Outlook
factors.summary: Oracle's massive $95B capex plan and negative FCF raise concerns about AI spending sustainability and ROI. Hyperscaler capex cycle may be topping; Anthropic seeking data center leases, suggesting infrastructure build-out is extending but profitability unclear.

factors.name: Retail Order Flow & Positioning
factors.summary: Options volatility elevated: Starbucks options jumped to 60k contracts, General Dynamics to 3.7k; retail chasing SpaceX IPO on risk-on sentiment post-Iran deal. Positive flow into equities on de-escalation relief.

factors.name: VIX / Volatility
factors.summary: Geopolitical tail risk (Iran) suddenly removed; VIX likely to compress lower. Lower vol typically supports growth/tech multiple expansion and risk-on positioning; reduces hedging demand.

key_levels.resistance: 29350 (intraday high post-Iran news, prior technical resistance)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq rallied +1.35% intraday on Trump's surprise Iran deal announcement, removing geopolitical war premium and lifting risk appetite sharply. However, same-session hot PPI data (1.1% vs 0.7% expected) has reintroduced inflation concerns and threatens to delay Fed rate cuts, keeping nominal and real yields sticky. Oracle's earnings-driven selloff on capex concerns signals underlying doubt about AI ROI and tech profitability durability. SpaceX IPO tomorrow is both a potential source of FOMO-driven buying and tail-risk capital allocation event; retail positioning is stretched. The move is underway (Phase 2) but faces near-term resistance from Fed uncertainty and persistent inflation data.
catalysts_ahead: SpaceX IPO launch (Friday ~morning US session) — potential market circuit-breaker or allocation event
US Consumer Sentiment & Rig Count data (Friday)
Children's Place earnings (Friday)
FISA Section 702 surveillance authority expires (Friday) — regulatory tail risk for tech
Fed speakers / ECB follow-up (ongoing commentary)
Next CPI/PCE release (mid-June) — inflation trajectory critical
screening.risk_warning: If PPI/inflation surprises continue higher or Fed signals further hawkishness, real yields could re-spike, compressing long-duration tech multiples and invalidating the post-Iran rally. Conversely, if SpaceX IPO triggers massive redemptions from other mega-caps or retail margin calls occur, downside acceleration into support is possible. Oracle's capex concerns could spread to MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN guidance if hyperscaler ROI questions surface at their earnings. Geopolitical risks remain elevated despite deal announcement (Iran retaliation threats against Musk's companies noted in news).
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway post-Iran announcement; avoid chasing at intraday highs. Watch for pullback to 29000–29100 support zone on any profit-taking or PPI inflation anxiety re-emergence before considering fresh long positioning. Monitor 10Y yield: if it breaks above 4.40%, real yields spike and de-rates tech — exit long ideas.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk-Off (Iran Deal)
factors.summary: Trump announced US-Iran peace deal cancelling planned strikes; markets surged with S&P 500 +1.3%, Nasdaq +1.8%, Russell 2000 +2.4%. Risk premium collapse lifts equities broadly, especially growth/tech.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: DXY retreated sharply from 3-month highs; USD fell most in >1 month post-Iran deal. Weaker dollar supports USD-revenue-heavy Nasdaq constituents (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN).

factors.name: Treasury Yields Under Pressure
factors.summary: 10Y bond auction tailed 1.2 bps (bad sign for demand), suggesting yield compression. Lower nominal yields typically support long-duration growth multiples in NAS100.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Euphoria + Capital Flows
factors.summary: SpaceX raised record $75B at $135/share, debuting Friday; generates massive retail FOMO and capital rotation into growth/tech. Musk's stature and Nasdaq listing catalyst keep momentum alive.

factors.name: Earnings-Driven Optimism (Selective)
factors.summary: Adobe raised FY guidance (post-market positive); RH beat and raised 2026 outlook; but Oracle tumbled 11% on capex concerns and negative FCF. Mixed bag, not uniform mega-cap strength.

factors.name: Semiconductor Tailwinds
factors.summary: S&P Global upgraded NVDA to AA on 'explosive AI demand'; ASML/chip supply chain commentary remains constructive. NAS100 has ~30% semiconductor weighting.

factors.name: Real Yields / TIPS Not Yet Tested
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS or CPI data in news window; real yield impact unclear, but nominal yield compression is helping. Inflation expectations remain embedded.

factors.name: Move Already In Progress (4h +1.42%, 24h +2.12%)
factors.summary: Rally has already run 2%+ in 24h post-Iran deal announcement. Price action is in Phase 2/3 territory; risk of near-term pullback/consolidation before resumption.

factors.name: Fed Policy / Upcoming Data
factors.summary: No imminent FOMC, CPI, or NFP in next 24-48h per calendar. Next high-impact catalyst likely early next week; reduces near-term volatility risk.

factors.name: Macro Headwinds (Capex Concerns, Valuations)
factors.summary: Oracle's $95B capex plan and FCF deterioration signal potential sustainability concerns in mega-cap AI spending cycle. Valuations on NAS100 remain stretched; resets possible if growth slows.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 surged +2.12% over 24h on Trump's Iran peace deal announcement, eliminating near-term geopolitical risk premium and weakening the USD. Growth equities benefited from lower nominal yields and de-risking sentiment, while SpaceX's record $75B IPO Friday fuels retail enthusiasm for tech/growth plays. However, the move is already materially underway (Phase 2), and Oracle's capex concerns signal caution around the AI spending super-cycle's sustainability. The next major catalyst (CPI, earnings, or Fed speak) is days away; expect consolidation or pullback before next leg.
catalysts_ahead: SpaceX Nasdaq debut Friday 6/12 — potential retail FOMO rotation
US economic data early next week (CPI, jobless claims, manufacturing)
Fed speakers mid-week (any hawkish surprises could reverse yield compression)
Mega-cap earnings (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, META, AMZN, NVDA) — guidance on AI capex critical
Treasury auctions (demand/yields signal real-rate expectations)
China macro data (any weakness supports risk-off, headwind for NDX)
screening.risk_warning: Iran deal falls apart or stalls (market reprices geopolitical risk back in, yields spike, USD rebounds). OR: Hawkish Fed speaker or hot inflation print flips yields higher, compressing NAS100 multiples. OR: SpaceX IPO flops / retail demand disappoints, reversing euphoria flow. OR: Oracle-style capex/FCF revisions become contagion across mega-caps (MSFT, AMZN cloud divisions). Any of these would invalidate the bullish setup and trigger 1-3% correction.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended; consider waiting for pullback to 29200–29400 support before adding longs. Alternatively, enter on break above 29800 with tight stop, but risk/reward is unfavorable given Phase 2 status. Short bias would require yield spike or deal announcement collapse.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already underway (+2.12% 24h); consider scaling in on pullback to 29200-29250 support or after consolidation confirmation above 29400.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal Announced
factors.summary: Trump announced cancellation of scheduled Iran strikes and approval of deal by all parties including Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Israel (per N12). Removes major geopolitical risk premium; bank stocks hit record highs on deal optimism per Bloomberg. S&P up 1.3%, Nasdaq up 1.8%, Russell +2.4% on announcement.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Mega-Event
factors.summary: SpaceX raised $75B in largest IPO ever at $135/share, $1.77T valuation, trading starts Friday on Nasdaq per Bloomberg. Massive retail and institutional enthusiasm; prediction markets expect >$2T close. Creates positive sentiment halo across tech sector and validates extreme valuations.

factors.name: Risk-On Sentiment Surge
factors.summary: Dollar dropped most in a month per Bloomberg; VIX likely compressed on geopolitical de-escalation. Oil near 8-week lows on Iran deal removes energy cost headwind. Strong rotation into risk assets with Russell 2000 outperforming suggests broad-based rally not just mega-cap.

factors.name: NVDA Credit Upgrade
factors.summary: S&P Global Ratings upgraded Nvidia to AA on explosive AI demand per news feed. Direct positive for largest NDX component by weight; validates AI capex cycle continuation thesis.

factors.name: ECB Rate Hike
factors.summary: ECB hiked rates per news headline; euro up, dollar down. Divergence in monetary policy could pressure Fed to maintain higher-for-longer stance, though immediate market reaction is risk-on due to Iran deal dominating.

factors.name: Tech Sector Mixed Earnings
factors.summary: Adobe raised annual forecasts but CFO exit creates uncertainty per news. RH beat and lifted outlook despite tariffs. Oracle tumbled 11% on cash concerns and increased capital raise. Mixed signals but no systemic sector concern.

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Real Rates
factors.summary: 30-year bond auction showed 1.2bp tail (weak demand) but immediate market focus on risk-on rally. Lower geopolitical premium likely supports bonds near-term; declining real yields positive for long-duration growth multiples in NDX.

factors.name: US PPI Data
factors.summary: Core PPI rose 0.4% MoM in May, holding at 4.9% YoY per FXStreet. Sticky inflation data argues against Fed cuts, but markets ignoring this on geopolitical optimism currently. Dollar initially hit 10-week high before reversing on Iran news.

factors.name: Tech IPO Sentiment
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO spawning leveraged ETFs (per MarketWatch); retail FOMO building. Forbright (Delaney SPAC) valued at $900M in Nasdaq debut. IPO window reopening is historically bullish for growth/tech sectors and validates private market valuations.

factors.name: Bank Stocks at Records
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports US bank stocks hit all-time highs on Iran deal hopes and IPO optimism. Financials strength suggests broad market health and credit availability for tech capex cycles.

confirmation.narrative: NAS100 surging +2.12% in 24h on explosive combination of US-Iran peace deal removing geopolitical risk premium and SpaceX $75B mega-IPO validating extreme tech valuations. Geopolitical de-escalation triggered broad risk-on rotation with dollar dropping most in a month, oil collapsing to 8-week lows, and banks hitting records. NVDA upgrade to AA and IPO window reopening provide structural tailwinds. Sticky PPI inflation data (Core +4.9% YoY) temporarily ignored but remains medium-term headwind for Fed cuts. Move is Phase 2 (already +1.42% in 4h) so immediate chase risky, but momentum and sentiment remain strongly bullish barring sudden deal collapse.
catalysts_ahead: SpaceX trading debut Friday on Nasdaq - sentiment barometer
Iran deal finalization over weekend - any reversal would spike vol
China May economic data (mentioned for Friday Asia session)
Next CPI/PCE print for Fed rate path clarity
Any Fed speaker commentary on persistent PPI inflation
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidates if: (1) Iran deal collapses or fails to be signed as claimed - would spike geopolitical premium and reverse risk-on trades; (2) SpaceX IPO disappoints on Friday creating tech sentiment contagion; (3) Fed speakers push back hawkishly on sticky PPI data; (4) NDX fails to hold 29200 support suggesting profit-taking overwhelming bullish catalysts. Phase 2 move means short-term pullback likely before continuation.

reason: FRESH high-conf read (7/10) — informational only, needs confirmation

factors.name: Geopolitical de-escalation (US-Iran)
factors.summary: Trump cancelled planned strikes and signaled deal near completion with possible weekend signing. Risk-off premium unwind favors equities and reduces safe-haven demand, supporting growth assets like Nasdaq.

factors.name: Oil price collapse
factors.summary: WTI dropped to $85 (two-month low) on Iran de-escalation news. Lower energy costs reduce inflation pressure and improve corporate margins, typically bullish for equities and growth multiples.

factors.name: USD weakness
factors.summary: DXY retreated from three-month high to ~99.60 after Trump's Iran announcement. Weaker dollar supports multinationals' international revenue translation and reduces headwinds for NQ-heavy tech with global exposure.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO ($75B, largest ever)
factors.summary: Historic $75B raise at $135/share, set to list Friday on Nasdaq with potential $1.77T+ market cap. Mega-IPO inflow and risk-on sentiment boost Nasdaq appetite; however, retail allocation at only low-20% range limits retail FOMO buying.

factors.name: Bank stocks rally to record highs
factors.summary: Financials hit all-time highs on Iran deal hopes and SpaceX IPO optimism (Bloomberg Markets, 20:54). De-escalation lifts risk appetite broadly; financial strength supports broader market confidence.

factors.name: Real yields and Treasury dynamics (mixed)
factors.summary: Bond yields fell on de-escalation risk-off unwind, but no major fresh inflation data in this window. Long-duration tech (NQ-heavy) should benefit from lower real yields, but magnitude unclear without CPI confirmation.

factors.name: Tech executive selling and Adobe CFO exit
factors.summary: Multiple insider sells (FormFactor CEO, Cisco EVP/CFO, Expensify leadership), and Adobe CFO exit fanning strategy concerns despite outlook raise. Insider pessimism amid strong equity rally is a subtle warning signal; Adobe stock at seven-year low.

factors.name: Oracle stock tumble (-11% on capex raise, negative FCF)
factors.summary: Oracle reported higher capex ($95B plan) and negative free cash flow, rattling investors despite earnings beat (MarketWatch, 20:25). Major Nasdaq component weakness and AI infrastructure spending concerns undermine tech euphoria.

factors.name: Bitcoin ETF outflows ($1.9B) and BTC below support
factors.summary: Spot Bitcoin ETF saw $1.9B outflows; BTC failing as hedge and at risk of breaching $60K (Cointelegraph, 22:58). Tech-BTC correlation and crypto weakness suggest risk-off undertone despite equity rally.

factors.name: Iran deal unconfirmed / Tehran pushback
factors.summary: Bloomberg and Investinglive note Iran not on Trump's list of signatories and disputed scope of US framing (22:55, 22:12). Tanker reportedly blocked from Hormuz; deal is Schrödinger's cat (signed and unsigned). High execution risk.

factors.name: Move already underway (4h +1.42%, 24h +2.12%)
factors.summary: Rally has already occurred; price action suggests Phase 2 extension rather than fresh Phase 1 breakout. Momentum intact but pullback risk elevated as euphoria prices in uncertain outcomes.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq rallied +2.12% in 24h on Trump's surprise US-Iran de-escalation announcement, which triggered a broad risk-on unwind (oil crashed to $85, USD weakened, financials hit record highs). SpaceX's record $75B IPO adding to equity appetite and Nasdaq rotation optimism. However, the deal itself remains unconfirmed by Tehran, Iran has disputed the framing, and a tanker was reportedly blocked—leaving room for reversal if headlines sour. Simultaneously, Oracle's -11% plunge on capex concerns and insider selling across tech names (Cisco, FormFactor, Adobe CFO exit) signal underlying caution despite headline rally. Move has already extended >2% intraday, suggesting early Phase 2 conditions where pullback entries are preferred.
catalysts_ahead: SpaceX IPO trading debut Friday (Nasdaq)
Trump-Iran deal finalization/signing (expected weekend or within days, high execution risk)
Potential Iran deal collapse or geopolitical re-escalation news
Fed speakers and rate expectations (ECB sources hint July pause if energy stays down)
Next CPI/PCE release (inflation data could reset bond yields and growth multiples)
Mega-cap tech earnings (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA) — typically move NQ 2-3%
Section 702 FISA surveillance authority expiration Friday (regulatory overhang)
screening.risk_warning: Iran deal collapses or geopolitical escalates → oil spikes, USD rallies, equities sell off. Fed signals hawkish pivot on inflation surprise → real yields spike, growth multiples compress, NQ vulnerable. Oracle weakness spreads to cloud/AI peers (MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA) → sector rotation into value. SpaceX IPO disappoints post-listing or Elon-related headline shock. Insider selling momentum builds into weakness. Move already +2%+ extended; late entrants risk sharp retest if Iran headlines sour or tech earnings disappoint.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (extended, 2%+ rally underway). Avoid chasing here; instead, wait for intraday pullback to 29300–29400 support before considering fresh long. If Iran headlines improve materially (confirmed Tehran signature), retest of resistance 29600–29800 becomes viable. Short-term traders: look for pullback entry; swing traders: wait for SpaceX IPO close Friday and next CPI before committing.
